rationale: Reconfirmed at c92b3d26 (worktree clean, no drift). The only round-2 change is a one-line tightening in TestLoad_EmptyStringModel subtest (b): it now additionally asserts the error message contains the distinctive phrase 'sets neither "model" nor "family"', uniquely pinning the neither-set validation branch so a fall-through to the count->=1 branch cannot pass spuriously. Both subtests PASS, `go test ./internal/config` is green, and `go build ./...` exits 0. This strictly strengthens round-1's already-approved behavior with no semantic or API changes. No new concerns.
findings: SHA c92b3d2650aaef05a4e4750e407ef910afba6ad7 confirmed via git rev-parse HEAD; git status --porcelain clean before and after review.
TestLoad_EmptyStringModel: both subtests (empty model with family resolves to family; empty model with no family is still refused) PASS.

New assertion in subtest (b) checks strings.Contains(msg, `sets neither "model" nor "family"`) in addition to the existing panel.reviewers[0] and recovery: checks — correctly narrows the test to the exact validateReviewerEntries branch.
No regressions or scope creep observed relative to round 1's codebase-pin lens.
